resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def fizzBuzz(self, n: int) -> List[str]:
        return ["FizzBuzz" if i % 15 == 0 else "Fizz" if i % 3 == 0 else "Buzz" if i % 5 == 0 else str(i) for i in range(1, n + 1)]
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses list comprehension to generate the FizzBuzz sequence in a single line of code. It leverages the properties of modular arithmetic to efficiently determine whether each number is divisible by 3, 5, or both, resulting in a time complexity of O(n). The space complexity is also O(n) due to the generated list.
